revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Iizaka Onsen]][[ja:飯坂温泉]] {{short description|Hot spring resort in Fukushima Prefecture, Japan}} {{For|the railway station|Iizaka Onsen Station}} {{Infobox spring | name = Iizaka Onsen
飯坂温泉 | photo = Iizaka Onsen 03.JPG | photo_width = | photo_caption = Iizaka Onsen | map = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_width = | map_caption = | name_origin = | location = [[:en:Fukushima, Fukushima|Fukushima, Fukushima]], Japan | elevation = | coordinates = {{coord|37.832597|140.452181|format=dms|display=inline|type:landmark_region:JP_scale:10000}} | coords_ref = | hot_spring_type = | discharge = | temperature = 42 deg C | depth = }} [[file:Exterior Shot of Sabakoyu Onsen.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Sabakoyu]] {{nihongo|'''Iizaka Onsen'''|飯坂温泉|}} is an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} (hot spring resort) located in the Iizaka district (former town of {{ill|Iizaka, Fukushima|en|Iizaka, Fukushima|qid=Q5995051|lt=Iizaka|1=Iizaka, Fukushima}}) within the city of [[:en:Fukushima, Fukushima|Fukushima]], Japan. It is located to the northwest of the city center, and is connected to {{ill|Fukushima Station (Fukushima)|en|Fukushima Station (Fukushima)|qid=Q800799|lt=Fukushima Station|1=Fukushima Station (Fukushima)}} by the {{ill|Fukushima Kōtsū Iizaka Line|en|Fukushima Kōtsū Iizaka Line|qid=Q5507732|lt=Fukushima Kōtsū Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Kōtsū Iizaka Line}} railroad. ==Description== Iizaka traditional hot spring town features over 40 traditional ''{{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|qid=Q562930|lt=ryokan|1=Ryokan}}'', and 9 public baths, including one of Japan’s oldest community bathhouses, '''Sabakoyu''' (鯖湖湯 or "Mackerel Lake Baths").Remembering the Spa (brochure). Sabakoyu was originally spelled 佐波来湯 when, according to legend, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, prince of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=imperial house|1=Imperial House of Japan}} and son of semi-legendary 12th {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, visited the area and was cured of his sickness after bathing in the hot springs. {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|qid=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}}, the famous {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} poet, visited Sabakoyu in 1689. ==References== *{{Citation |date=2006|title= Iizaka Onsen, Meiji Era Community - Remembering the Spa (brochure)|place=Fukushima City|publisher=Fukushima City Touring Promotion Co.}} {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.fckk.co.jp/ Fukushima sightseeing development incorporated company] [[en:Iizaka Onsen]] {{wikidata link|Q17192714}} [[Category:Fukushima (city)]] [[Category:Hot springs of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
